info.name: CognIX AI — Auth
info.description:
Authentication flows for CognIX AI.

## Two ways to authenticate

### 1. Email OTP (passwordless)
- Step 1 → `POST /auth/otp/send` — send 6-digit OTP to email
- Step 2 → `POST /auth/otp/verify` — verify OTP → receive JWT tokens

### 2. Clerk OAuth (Google, GitHub, etc.)
- User completes OAuth on the frontend via Clerk SDK
- Frontend receives a Clerk session token
- Step 1 → `POST /auth/clerk/sync` — exchange Clerk token → receive JWT tokens

## Token usage
All protected endpoints require:
```
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
```

## Collection variables
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `baseUrl` | API base URL, e.g. `http://localhost:3000/api/v1` |
| `accessToken` | Set automatically after OTP verify / Clerk sync |
| `refreshToken` | Set automatically after OTP verify / Clerk sync |

variable.description: Base URL of the CognIX API
JWT access token — populated automatically by test scripts
Refresh token — populated automatically by test scripts

item.name: Flow 1 — Email OTP
item.description:
Passwordless registration and login using a 6-digit code sent to the user's email.

The same two endpoints handle both new users (registration) and returning users (login) — the backend detects which case applies automatically.

request.description:
Sends a 6-digit OTP to the provided email address.

- Works for **both new and existing users** — no need for separate register/login endpoints on the client.
- `niche_id` is **optional**. Pass it here if the user selected a professional niche on the guest screen before hitting the prompt limit. It will be saved to the account on successful verification.
- The OTP expires in **10 minutes**.
- Calling this endpoint again before verification invalidates the previous code.

**Response fields**
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `message` | string | Confirmation message |
| `isNewUser` | boolean | `true` if no account exists yet for this email |

response.name: New user — OTP sent
Existing user — OTP sent

request.description:
Verifies the 6-digit OTP.

**New user path** — account is created, niche is saved, tokens returned.
**Existing user path** — email is confirmed (if not already), niche attached if missing, tokens returned.

`niche_id` here takes precedence over the one passed in step 1 — or you can send it only here if the user selects it after the OTP is sent.

**Error cases**
| HTTP | Reason |
|---|---|
| 400 | OTP not found / already used |
| 400 | OTP expired (10-minute window) |
| 400 | OTP code is wrong |

**Response fields**
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `user` | object | Sanitized user profile (no password) |
| `accessToken` | string | JWT — 7-day default |
| `refreshToken` | string | Opaque token — 30-day default |
| `expiresIn` | string | Access token TTL, e.g. `"7d"` |

response.name: Error — OTP expired

item.name: Flow 2 — Clerk OAuth
item.description:
OAuth login via Clerk (Google, GitHub, Apple, etc.).

**How it works**
1. The frontend uses the Clerk SDK / hosted UI to complete OAuth.
2. Clerk returns a session token (JWT).
3. The frontend calls `POST /auth/clerk/sync` with that token.
4. The backend verifies the token with Clerk's API, then finds or creates the matching user in the CognIX database and issues its own JWT pair.

**Account merging**: If a user previously signed up via email OTP with the same address, the Clerk identity is silently linked to the existing account — no duplicate accounts.

request.description:
Exchange a Clerk session token for CognIX JWT tokens.

This is the **only** endpoint needed after the frontend Clerk OAuth flow completes.

**Account resolution order**
1. Look up existing user by `clerk_user_id` (fastest path — returning OAuth user)
2. Look up by email (merges with an existing email-OTP account and links the Clerk ID)
3. Create a new user (brand-new OAuth user)

`niche_id` is optional — only used when creating a brand-new account. Ignored for existing accounts.

**Error cases**
| HTTP | Reason |
|---|---|
| 400 | Clerk integration not configured (`CLERK_SECRET_KEY` missing) |
| 400 | Clerk account has no verified email |
| 401 | Clerk token is invalid or expired |

**Response fields**
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `user` | object | Sanitized user profile |
| `accessToken` | string | CognIX JWT |
| `refreshToken` | string | CognIX refresh token |
| `expiresIn` | string | Access token TTL |

item.name: Session Management
item.description: Token refresh and logout — identical regardless of whether the user authenticated via OTP or Clerk OAuth.

request.description:
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token + refresh token pair.

- The **old refresh token is revoked** immediately.
- Use the new `refreshToken` from the response for the next rotation.
- Refresh tokens expire after **30 days** by default.

**Error cases**
| HTTP | Reason |
|---|---|
| 401 | Token not found or already revoked |
| 401 | Token expired |
| 401 | User account deleted |

Revokes the provided refresh token.

- Requires a valid `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` header.
- The access token itself will keep working until it naturally expires (7 days); the client should discard it.
- If the refresh token is already revoked the call still returns 200 (idempotent).

Returns the currently authenticated user's profile as decoded from the JWT.

Useful for verifying a token is still valid and retrieving live user data without a full DB lookup on the client.

**Response fields**
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | User UUID |
| `email` | string | Email address |
| `name` | string\|null | Display name |
| `emailVerified` | boolean | Always `true` for OTP/OAuth users |
| `authProvider` | string | `email_otp` or `clerk_oauth` |
| `onboardingStatus` | string | `pending` \| `in_progress` \| `completed` |
| `subscriptionTier` | string | `free` \| `premium` |
| `avatar` | string\|null | Avatar URL |
